title: Radio emergency message format
summary: Send call sign or identity, exact location, time, situation, assistance needed, hazards, people affected, and when you will listen again.
content: Send call sign or identity, exact location, time, situation, assistance needed, hazards, people affected, and when you will listen again. Repeat critical numbers and locations and ask the receiver to read them back. Do not transmit private medical details or names on an open channel unless necessary for care.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Radio emergency message format”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Radio emergency message format»
steps: Send call sign or identity, exact location, time, situation, assistance needed, hazards, people affected, and when you will listen again.
Repeat critical numbers and locations and ask the receiver to read them back.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for Radio emergency message format.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for Radio emergency message format.
warnings: Do not transmit private medical details or names on an open channel unless necessary for care.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for Radio emergency message format.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for Radio emergency message format.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during Radio emergency message format.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for Radio emergency message format.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for Radio emergency message format.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during Radio emergency message format.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Radio emergency message format.
short_term: For Radio emergency message format, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Radio emergency message format, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Radio emergency message format, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Radio emergency message format to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
sources: CDC — Water, sanitation and hygiene emergencies: https://www.cdc.gov/water-emergency/index.html
